summary: sensivity ships a game-cheat with hidden persistence, process masquerade, and obfuscated remote-licensed payload
details: On require/run, launcher.js installs an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run autostart entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at a bundled VBS (`OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs`) which silently relaunches `node launcher.js` window-hidden at every login — persistence disguised as a Microsoft component that survives package uninstall. Both the supervisor and worker children set `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` (a real Windows system process) and are spawned `detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true`, so the running payload is hidden from Task Manager. launcher.js then `eval()`s a 128KB obfuscator.io-mangled `server.obf.js` (626-entry shuffled string array, RC4-style decoder, control-flow flattening) which loads a 6.8MB native module `sens.node`. `sens.node` contains strings 'Aimbot', 'Triggerbot', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', '__licenseAccepted'; `public/index.html` advertises a 'Sensivity' cheat panel with Aim/ESP/Vehicle Spawn/Weapon controls — the package is a FiveM/GTA game cheat masquerading as a generic 'Control Panel' library. The supervisor runs PowerShell `Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 3000... Stop-Process -Force` to kill any other process listening on TCP/3000, taking the port from the user's other services. A monitor loop writes `.yt.ps1` to the package directory every 3 seconds and runs `powershell -NoProfile -EP Bypass -File` against it to enumerate every browser window title for 'YouTube'; a second `.qrshow.ps1` script is dropped to spawn a hidden console. ExecutionPolicy is explicitly bypassed. The eval'd `server.obf.js` collects an HWID derived from `HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid`, `os.hostname()`, and `Get-CimInstance Win32_ComputerSystemProduct` (CSProductId / volume serial), and POSTs the hash plus license key to a hardcoded URL stitched together from the obfuscation string array as 'https://iz...' — destination concealed by design.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
